moves.note: Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety
Exit all leveraged ETFs for safety

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet single name limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing capital preservation.
Reduce CRWD to 10% to further diversify and manage single name risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% for a stronger income component and stability.
Buy XLF at 5% to add diversification into a new, strong sector.
Establish a 15% cash buffer for enhanced capital preservation and flexibility.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5% monthly return, focusing on capital preservation and stability. Expected drawdowns are significantly reduced, but growth potential is limited. This mode minimizes leverage and maintains a substantial cash buffer.

moves.note: Add quality tech with good score

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with concentration limits and manage broad index exposure.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its below-stop status and high leverage.
Reduce SPAL to 5% to keep leveraged ETF exposure small and within limits.
Trim SMH to 10% given its downtrend, reallocating capital.
Buy PANW at 8% and MSFT at 7% to add high-quality, high-scoring tech names.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5% monthly growth by managing risk first, blending broad index with quality tech. Leverage is reduced, and concentration is managed. Expected swings are moderate, with a focus on sustainable growth.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, reallocating capital to higher growth areas.
Sell SOXL entirely as it's below stop, re-deploying capital into stronger setups.
Reduce SMH to 10% due to its downtrend, focusing on uptrending assets.
Add to CRWD, increasing exposure to a strong uptrending winner.
Buy PANW at 12% and NVDU at 10% to lean into high-scoring, high-reward setups.
Maintain a smaller 5% cash buffer to maximize deployed capital for growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% monthly growth by leaning into high-scoring setups and current winners. It uses leverage more extensively, which amplifies both potential upside and downside. Expect significantly larger drawdowns and be aware of leveraged ETF decay; strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce growth exposure and shift towards income.
Add to QQQI, increasing its allocation to 30% to maximize monthly distributions.
Trim CRWD to 5% and sell SMH to minimize high-beta single name and sector exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to completely eliminate leveraged ETFs and reduce volatility.
Establish a 15% cash buffer for enhanced stability and liquidity.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% monthly distributions, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable income over growth. NAV can still fall in a selloff, and distributions are variable. This mode is designed for cash-like income, not a savings account, with minimal growth-oriented assets.
